# SRC020 - Know Your Customers’ Jobs to Be Done

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Software Adoption |
| Authors / source | Christensen et al. |
| Year | 2016 |
| Source type | HBR practitioner theory |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://hbr.org/2016/09/know-your-customers-jobs-to-be-done](https://hbr.org/2016/09/know-your-customers-jobs-to-be-done) |

## Bias / limitation

Practitioner framing; excellent for interviews.

## Core takeaway

Customers hire products to make progress in a situation; jobs have functional, social, and emotional dimensions.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to mine buyer language and write ads around progress, not features.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown

- What it proves:

- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
